name: Salesforce
what: Salesforce is the leading cloud-based CRM platform offering Sales Cloud, Service Cloud, Marketing Cloud, and more, with extensive REST/SOAP/Bulk/Streaming APIs for data and automation.
auth: OAuth 2.0 (Connected App with client_id/client_secret); supports password grant, authorization code, and JWT flows. Access tokens obtained via standard OAuth endpoints.
access_note: Free developer account and free CRM trials available at developer.salesforce.com and salesforce.com/crm/free-trial with no gating required.
api: Yes — REST API, SOAP API, Bulk API, Streaming API, Metadata API, and Account Engagement (Pardot) API; documented at developer.salesforce.com/docs
mcp: Official vendor MCP — Salesforce provides Hosted MCP Servers (developer.salesforce.com/docs/platform/hosted-mcp-servers) and the Salesforce DX MCP Server (github.com/salesforcecli/mcp), both vendor-run.
build: Straightforward: create a free developer org, configure a Connected App for OAuth 2.0, obtain credentials, and call REST/SOAP endpoints. Extensive official docs and SDKs available.
blocker: Production orgs require a paid Salesforce subscription; API access may be rate-limited or restricted depending on edition (e.g., Essentials tier has limited API calls). Connected App approval may require admin privileges in enterprise orgs.
ev: Multiple official Salesforce developer docs snippets confirm OAuth 2.0 auth, free trial/developer access, and two distinct official MCP offerings (Hosted MCP Servers and Salesforce DX MCP Server).

name: Zendesk
what: Customer support and helpdesk platform with ticketing, live chat, CRM (Sell), and knowledge base products; REST APIs cover all major objects (tickets, users, organizations, chat, CRM).
auth: OAuth 2.0 (multiple grant types: authorization code, implicit, resource owner password) and API tokens (created in Admin Center); both supported across Zendesk Support, Chat, and Sell APIs.
access_note: Free 14-day trial available without contacting sales; paid plans from $19/month. Developer partner program offers sponsored accounts.
api: Fully public REST API documented at https://developer.zendesk.com/api-reference; covers Support, Chat, Sell/CRM, Guide, and Talk products.
mcp: Zendesk supports MCP as a client/consumer — Admin Center allows creating MCP server connections within Actions flows (support.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/10497779528730). Zendesk does not publish its own official MCP server; community/third-party servers exist (mattcoatsworth/zendesk-mcp-server, mcpmarket).
build: Integrate via REST API with OAuth2 or API token auth; use webhooks for event-driven flows; 1,200+ Marketplace integrations available. MCP integration (EAP) allows AI agents to use Zendesk tools via Model Context Protocol.
blocker: MCP native support is in Early Access Program (EAP) — requires opt-in. Production plans required for full API access beyond trial limits.
ev: Developer docs confirm OAuth2 and API token auth; pricing page confirms self-serve trial; TechRadar and Zendesk support article confirm official MCP adoption (EAP); community MCP servers confirmed on GitHub and LobeHub.

name: Slack
what: Team messaging and collaboration platform with channels, direct messages, file sharing, and app integrations
auth: OAuth 2.0 authorization code grant flow; also supports Bot tokens and legacy tokens
access_note: Free tier available; paid plans with free trial; API access available immediately upon app creation at api.slack.com
api: REST-based Web API (https://api.slack.com/web) plus Events API, RTM API, and Socket Mode
mcp: Official vendor MCP server exists; documented at https://docs.slack.dev/ai/slack-mcp-server and https://slack.com/help/articles/48855576908307-Guide-to-Model-Context-Protocol-in-Slack
build: Strong integration target: extensive REST API, webhooks, OAuth, Bolt SDKs (JS/Python/Java), well-documented developer portal at docs.slack.dev
blocker: None significant; API ToS must be accepted; rate limits apply per method tier
ev: Multiple official Slack developer docs pages confirm OAuth flow, Web API, and official MCP server; self-serve confirmed by free tier and trial pages

name: Google Ads
what: Google Ads API — programmatic interface for managing Google Ads accounts, campaigns, reporting, and ad operations at scale.
auth: OAuth 2.0 credentials (client ID/secret or service account) plus a developer token required for all API calls; manager account calls also require a login-customer-id header.
access_note: Developer token must be applied for and approved by Google (basic access granted quickly, standard access requires an audit). OAuth app credentials are self-provisioned via Google Cloud Console.
mcp: Official vendor MCP server — Google open-sourced the Google Ads MCP Server on GitHub (announced via Search Engine Land and YouTube); allows LLMs like Gemini/Claude to interact with the Ads API via natural language.
build: Yes — full CRUD API with campaign management, reporting, keyword/budget management, and more; client libraries available in multiple languages.
blocker: Developer token approval required (basic tier auto-approved for test accounts; standard production access needs Google review). OAuth consent screen may require verification for external apps.
ev: HIGH — multiple official Google developer docs, GitHub (google/ads-api-report-fetcher), official MCP announcement on Search Engine Land, and Google YouTube channel confirm all details.

name: Shopify
what: Shopify is a leading e-commerce platform providing APIs (Admin API, Storefront API, etc.) for building apps, managing stores, products, orders, and enabling commerce integrations.
auth: OAuth 2.0 for public/custom apps; API key + secret for private/custom app token exchange; HMAC for webhook verification
access_note: Developers can register at partners.shopify.com and obtain API credentials immediately; no gating required for standard API access
api: REST and GraphQL Admin API, Storefront API, Payments API, and others — documented at https://shopify.dev/docs/api
mcp: Official vendor MCP server exists — Shopify publishes 'Storefront MCP' documented at https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/storefront-mcp; also community/third-party MCP servers exist separately
build: Yes — full developer platform with REST/GraphQL APIs, webhooks, app extensions, Polaris UI kit, and CLI tooling
blocker: None for standard API access; app store listing requires Shopify partner review; billing API and certain features require app approval
ev: Official Shopify dev docs confirm OAuth 2.0 auth, Storefront MCP server (shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/storefront-mcp), and self-serve partner registration

name: DataForSEO
what: DataForSEO provides SEO and SERP data APIs covering keyword research, backlinks, on-page analysis, SERP scraping, and merchant data at pay-per-use pricing.
auth: HTTP Basic Auth using API login + auto-generated API password obtained from the account dashboard.
access_note: Free trial with $1 credit on signup; no approval required. Pay-as-you-go pricing per API call.
api: REST API v3 (async and live endpoints); docs at https://docs.dataforseo.com/v3/
mcp: Official vendor MCP server exists: github.com/dataforseo/mcp-server-typescript and Docker image dataforseo/mcp, documented at dataforseo.com/help-center/setting-up-the-official-dataforseo-mcp-server-simple-guide
build: Well-documented REST API with Basic Auth, pay-per-use model, SDKs implied, async batching supported, and official MCP server for AI integration.
blocker: None significant; free trial available immediately on registration.
ev: Multiple official DataForSEO docs and blog pages confirm self-serve signup, Basic Auth mechanism, pricing model, and vendor-run MCP server on GitHub and Docker Hub.

name: GitHub
what: GitHub is a cloud-based platform for version control, code collaboration, CI/CD, and developer workflows, built on Git. It offers REST and GraphQL APIs for programmatic access to repositories, issues, PRs, actions, and more.
auth: OAuth 2.0 (OAuth Apps and GitHub Apps), Personal Access Tokens (PAT), GitHub App installation tokens. OAuth requires registering an app to obtain a Client ID and Client Secret.
access_note: Free tier available at github.com/pricing with immediate API access; paid plans (Team, Enterprise Cloud) also self-serve. Enterprise Server is separately licensed.
api: Yes — comprehensive REST API (https://docs.github.com/rest) and GraphQL API (https://docs.github.com/graphql). Well-documented, versioned, widely used.
mcp: Official vendor MCP server exists — github-mcp-server. Went GA September 2025 (public preview April 2025). Supports remote (hosted, one-click OAuth 2.1 + PKCE) and local (Docker/Go binary) deployment; also supports PAT auth. See https://github.com/github/github-mcp-server
build: Straightforward. Register an OAuth App or GitHub App via GitHub settings, obtain credentials, use REST or GraphQL APIs. SDKs available (Octokit for JS, Python, Ruby, etc.). Official MCP server ready for AI agent integration.
blocker: None significant. Rate limits apply (5,000 req/hr authenticated for REST). Some enterprise features (GHAS, data residency) require paid plans.
ev: GitHub's own docs confirm OAuth/token auth, self-serve signup, and REST API. Augment Code MCP registry and GitHub Changelog confirm official github-mcp-server in public preview authored by GitHub.

name: Notion
what: Notion is a connected workspace for notes, docs, databases, and project management with a public REST API and official MCP server.
auth: Bearer token (API key / internal integration token) or OAuth 2.0 for public integrations
access_note: API is free on all plans; developers can create integrations immediately at developers.notion.com without sales approval
api: REST API (versioned, e.g. Notion-Version: 2026-03-11); bearer token required for all requests
mcp: Official vendor-hosted MCP server exists (Notion engineering blog post 'Notion's hosted MCP server: an inside look' + official docs at developers.notion.com/guides/mcp/build-mcp-client)
build: Full CRUD on pages, databases, blocks, comments, users via REST API; OAuth 2.0 for multi-tenant apps; official MCP for AI agent integration; webhooks not natively supported (polling pattern common)
blocker: Integrations require user to explicitly share specific Notion pages/databases with the integration; SCIM API restricted to Enterprise plan
ev: developers.notion.com quickstart docs, Notion engineering blog MCP post, Tray.ai connector docs showing API key + OAuth2 options, YouTube noting API is free on all plans

name: Stripe
what: Stripe is a payment processing and financial infrastructure platform offering APIs for payments, billing, subscriptions, identity, connect, and more.
auth: API Key (Bearer token, sk_test_* or sk_live_*) for direct API access; OAuth2 for Connect/Standard accounts; restricted keys for Stripe Apps
access_note: Sign up at stripe.com; API keys available immediately in dashboard; no approval required for test mode; live mode requires account activation
api: REST API, extensively documented at https://stripe.com/docs/api; official SDKs for Node, Python, Ruby, PHP, Java, Go, .NET
mcp: Official vendor MCP server exists; Stripe hosts a remote MCP server (confirmed via lobehub.com/mcp/stripe-stripe-mcp snippet referencing 'Stripe hosts a remote MCP server')
build: Extremely mature API with SDKs, webhooks, Stripe Apps marketplace, Connect platform, and comprehensive docs; Composio also supports Stripe auth configs
blocker: Live/production mode requires business verification and Stripe account approval; some features (e.g., Embedded Stripe, certain Connect capabilities) are gated or in private preview
ev: Multiple official Stripe docs snippets confirming API key and OAuth2 auth; vendor MCP server confirmed via third-party aggregator referencing Stripe-hosted remote MCP; Composio auth page confirms API Key and OAuth2 support

name: NotebookLM
what: NotebookLM is Google's AI-powered research and note-taking assistant that can ingest documents and generate summaries, Q&A, and audio overviews (podcasts). An enterprise tier (NotebookLM Enterprise) exposes an official REST API for notebook and source management.
auth: OAuth 2.0 / Google Cloud IAM (Bearer token via `gcloud auth print-access-token`); Cloud Identity or Google Workspace identity frameworks supported for Enterprise
access_note: Consumer NotebookLM is free/self-serve but has no public API. The REST API is only available through NotebookLM Enterprise, which requires purchasing licenses via Google Cloud sales contact.
api: Yes — NotebookLM Enterprise has an official REST API (notebooks.create and related methods documented at docs.cloud.google.com/gemini/enterprise/notebooklm-enterprise/docs/api-notebooks). No official public API for consumer tier.
mcp: Community/third-party only. Multiple community MCP servers exist (kkaminsk/NotebookLM-MCP on GitHub, moodrobotics/notebooklm-mcp-server, PleasePromptu on mcp.directory) but all use browser automation or unofficial methods — no official vendor-run MCP server found.
build: Feasible for Enterprise customers: use the official REST API with Google Cloud IAM auth to create/manage notebooks and sources. Consumer-tier automation requires unofficial workarounds (browser automation, community REST wrappers like gnh1201/notebooklm-rest-api) which are fragile and unsupported.
blocker: Enterprise API access requires purchasing NotebookLM Enterprise licenses through Google Cloud sales — not self-serve. Consumer tier has no official API. Community wrappers rely on session auth/browser automation and may break.
ev: Official Enterprise API docs at docs.cloud.google.com/gemini/enterprise/notebooklm-enterprise/docs/api-notebooks confirm REST API with Bearer token auth. Google Cloud enterprise page confirms sales-gated licensing. Community MCP servers confirmed on GitHub and MCP directories but none are vendor-official.
